title: Tuza's Conjecture
context_text: Tuza conjectured in 1981 that in a graph without $k+1$ edge-disjoint triangles, it suffices to delete at most $2k$ edges to obtain a triangle-free graph. Here $\mu(G)$ denotes the maximum size of a triangle packing and $\tau(G)$ the minimum size of a triangle hitting set. The conjecture is tight for $K_4$ and $K_5$, and is known to hold asymptotically for all graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves Tuza's Conjecture for threshold graphs (graphs that are both split graphs and cographs) and for even balanced co-chain graphs (co-chain graphs where both sides have the same size divisible by 4).
